bibliographic_data.title: SoK: A Systematic Review of TEE Usage for Developing Trusted Applications
bibliographic_data.abstract: Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) are a feature of modern CPUs that aim to provide a high assurance, isolated environment in which to run workloads that demand both confidentiality and integrity. This article aims to analyse the TEE ecosystem, determine its usability, and suggest improvements where necessary to make adoption easier.

core_content.novelty_claim: The paper provides a systematic review of Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) usage for developing trusted applications, analyzing their usability and suggesting improvements.
core_content.key_methodology: The authors gathered academic and practical examples from a total of 223 references to summarize the literature on TEEs and provide insights into their evolution. They categorized TAs into major groups and analyzed tools available to developers, evaluated trusted container projects, tested performance, and identified requirements for migrating applications inside them.
outcomes: Categorized Trusted Applications (TAs) into major groups
Tested performance of TEEs and tcons
Identified requirements for migrating applications inside them
core_content.full_text_markdown: Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) are a feature of modern CPUs that aim to provide a high assurance, isolated environment in which to run workloads that demand both confidentiality and integrity. This article aims to analyze the TEE ecosystem, determine its usability, and suggest improvements where necessary to make adoption easier. To better understand TEE usage, we gathered academic and practical examples from 223 references. We summarize the literature and provide a publication timeline, along with insights into the evolution of TEE research and deployment. We categorize Trusted Applications (TAs) into major groups and analyze the tools available to developers. Lastly, we evaluate trusted container projects, test performance, and identify the requirements for migrating applications inside them.
